mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which mechanically increases present equity values and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top quintile of FEDFUNDS declines fails to outperform the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack weakens aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in both retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicates a coordinated contraction in aggregate demand and labor income, which jointly depress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of joint-decline months, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the cost of capital and compresses equity risk premiums, which directly reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals a rapid contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which precedes downward earnings revisions and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of INDPRO declines exceeding 1% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding aggregate output that translates into higher nominal corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns turns negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the top GDP-growth quintile fails to outperform the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in CPIAUCSL combined with an unchanged FEDFUNDS narrows the real policy rate and forces equity investors to discount higher future inflation volatility, which raises the equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and flat or falling FEDFUNDS coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumer spending power, which drives higher corporate sales and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the top quintile of payroll changes fails to beat the bottom quintile.
